JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Nov-16-2020 Continued To Nov-18-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 306
MINI MINUTES: This proceeding is occasioned for those prospective jurors ostensibly unable to complete either the hardship form or questionnaire form on-line. Court and court staff are present. All counsel and parties were excused from appearance.

25 prospective jurors are present and placed under oath. The Court describes the process for the morning. 10 prospective jurors complete the hardship form and are excused after review. 14 jurors complete the questionnaire. One prospective juror was able to complete the questionnaire on line while seated in the jury assembly room.

14 juror questionnaires are retrieved by Plaintiff's counsel for copying and distribution. All original questionnaires are returned in the afternoon.

Judge: Hon. Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Not Reported. (D306)

Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Jul-30-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Motion For Summary Judgmen is denied. The City's alternative summary adjudication motion is granted in part and denied in part. The motion has two grounds:

First, it says San Francisco Municipal Railway (aka Muni) is not a proper defendant because it is a City department and thus lacks capacity to be sued. Plaintiff Valorie Lemley now agrees. Summary adjudication is granted on this issue.

Second, the City says it is not liable on plaintiff's sole cause of action, general negligence, because its bus driver acted with appropriate care. It is undisputed that, on January 13, 2018, plaintiff fell on a Muni bus from a standing position when the bus slowed for a traffic light. (OSS 4, 10-12.) Based largely on bus video, plaintiff's expert opines that she fell due to sudden deceleration caused by the driver's excessive braking, from which causation can be readily inferred. (Gayner Dec.) Reviewing the same video, a City expert opines that the driver made a proper stop. (Haletky Dec.) This core dispute is for a trier of fact; summary adjudication is denied. (CCP 437c.) Whether plaintiff may have comparative fault is also for a trier of fact.

order is signed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
